userStories.title: Split session.ts below 400-line limit
userStories.description:
Extract the 873-line `src/debate/session.ts` into focused modules while preserving all existing behavior and barrel exports.

1. **session-helpers.ts** â€” Shared types (`ResolvedDebater`, `SuccessfulProposal`, `ResolveOutcome`), helper functions (`buildFailedResult`, `runComplete`, `modelTierFromDebater`, `isTierLabel`), `_debateSessionDeps`, `resolveDebaterModel`, `DebateSessionOptions`, and constants (`RESOLVER_FALLBACK_AGENT`, `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`).
2. **session-one-shot.ts** â€” `runOneShot()` extracted as a standalone function (or method mixin) that receives `this` context from DebateSession.
3. **session-stateful.ts** â€” `runStateful()`, `runStatefulTurn()`, `closeStatefulSession()` extracted similarly.
4. **session-plan.ts** â€” `runPlan()` logic extracted.
5. **session.ts** â€” Thin `DebateSession` class: constructor, `run()` dispatcher, `resolve()`, and delegation to extracted modules.
6. **index.ts** â€” Updated barrel exports (must remain identical public API).
acceptanceCriteria: After split, no file in src/debate/ exceeds 400 lines
DebateSession.run() with sessionMode 'one-shot' delegates to the extracted runOneShot logic and returns identical DebateResult as before
DebateSession.run() with sessionMode 'stateful' delegates to the extracted runStateful logic and returns identical DebateResult as before
DebateSession.runPlan() delegates to the extracted runPlan logic and returns identical DebateResult as before
_debateSessionDeps is exported from session-helpers.ts and re-exported through the barrel index.ts
resolveDebaterModel() is exported from session-helpers.ts and re-exported through the barrel index.ts
DebateSessionOptions type is exported from session-helpers.ts and re-exported through the barrel index.ts
All existing debate tests pass without modification to test files

userStories.title: Add DebateMode type, mode field to config schema and defaults
userStories.description: Add `DebateMode = 'panel' | 'hybrid'` type to `src/debate/types.ts`. Add `mode` field to `DebateStageConfig` interface. Add `mode: z.enum(['panel', 'hybrid']).default('panel')` to `DebateStageConfigSchema` in `src/config/schemas.ts`. Update all stage defaults in both the schema factory defaults and the inline default function (line 751 of schemas.ts) to include `mode: 'panel'`. Also add the `Rebuttal` interface and optional `rebuttals?: Rebuttal[]` to `DebateResult` â€” combined here since both are small type-only changes in the same file.
acceptanceCriteria: NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.plan.mode equals 'panel'
NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.review.mode equals 'panel'
NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.acceptance.mode equals 'panel'
NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.rectification.mode equals 'panel'
NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.escalation.mode equals 'panel'
NaxConfigSchema.parse({ debate: { stages: { plan: { mode: 'hybrid' } } } }) parses without error and stages.plan.mode equals 'hybrid'
NaxConfigSchema.safeParse({ debate: { stages: { plan: { mode: 'sequential' } } } }).success equals false
DebateStageConfig interface includes mode property typed as DebateMode
DebateResult interface includes optional rebuttals property typed as Rebuttal[]
Rebuttal interface has debater typed as Debater, round typed as number, and output typed as string
Existing code compiles without changes â€” mode and rebuttals fields are optional with defaults

userStories.title: Add DebateMode type, Rebuttal interface, and mode/rebuttals fields to debate types
userStories.description: In `src/debate/types.ts`: (1) export `DebateMode = 'panel' | 'hybrid'` type alias. (2) Add `mode: DebateMode` field to `DebateStageConfig` interface. (3) Add `Rebuttal` interface with fields `debater: Debater`, `round: number`, `output: string`. (4) Add optional `rebuttals?: Rebuttal[]` field to `DebateResult`. All additions are purely additive â€” no existing fields change, so downstream code continues to compile without modification.
acceptanceCriteria: DebateMode type is exported from src/debate/types.ts and equals the union 'panel' | 'hybrid'
DebateStageConfig interface has a mode property typed as DebateMode
Rebuttal interface is exported and has debater typed as Debater, round typed as number, and output typed as string
DebateResult interface has an optional rebuttals property typed as Rebuttal[]
All existing consumers of DebateStageConfig and DebateResult compile without changes (mode and rebuttals are additive)

routing.reasoning: Pure type-only additions to a single file â€” no runtime behavior, no logic, no imports added. Purely additive so existing code is unaffected. ~15 LOC.

userStories.title: Add mode field to DebateStageConfigSchema and update all stage defaults to 'panel'
userStories.description: In `src/config/schemas.ts`: (1) Add `mode: z.enum(['panel', 'hybrid']).default('panel')` to the `DebateStageConfigSchema` factory (line ~478). (2) Update the inline default function for `DebateConfigSchema.optional().default(...)` (line ~751) to include `mode: 'panel'` in every stage object (plan, review, acceptance, rectification, escalation). No other files change. The Zod schema change is the source of truth; the inline default object must mirror it so both code paths agree.

NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.review.mode equals 'panel'
NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.acceptance.mode equals 'panel'
NaxConfigSchema.parse({}).debate.stages.rectification.mode equals 'panel'

routing.reasoning: Single-file schema change (~10 LOC) with clear behavioral verification via NaxConfigSchema.parse() assertions. Inline default object update is mechanical. All 8 ACs are directly testable with bun:test. Depends on US-001-A for the DebateMode type to exist in the interface layer.

userStories.title: Route run() by mode with hybrid fallback to panel
userStories.description: Update the `run()` method in `DebateSession` (now in session.ts after split) to check `this.stageConfig.mode` before dispatching. When `mode === 'hybrid'` and `sessionMode === 'stateful'`, call `runHybrid()`. When `mode === 'hybrid'` but `sessionMode` is `'one-shot'` or undefined, log a warning and fall back to `runOneShot()`. When `mode === 'panel'` (default), preserve existing behavior. The `runHybrid` method will be a stub that throws `new Error('Not implemented')` until US-003 implements it â€” this allows testing the routing logic independently.
acceptanceCriteria: run() with mode 'panel' and sessionMode 'one-shot' calls runOneShot and returns its result
run() with mode 'panel' and sessionMode 'stateful' calls runStateful and returns its result
run() with mode undefined and sessionMode 'one-shot' calls runOneShot (backward compat â€” mode defaults to panel)
run() with mode 'hybrid' and sessionMode 'stateful' calls runHybrid
run() with mode 'hybrid' and sessionMode 'one-shot' calls runOneShot and logs a warning containing 'hybrid mode requires sessionMode: stateful'
run() with mode 'hybrid' and sessionMode undefined calls runOneShot and logs a warning containing 'hybrid mode requires sessionMode: stateful'

userStories.title: buildRebuttalContext prompt builder
userStories.description:
Add `buildRebuttalContext()` to `src/debate/prompts.ts` and export from the barrel. This function builds the prompt for a rebuttal turn: includes all proposals, all previous rounds' rebuttals (flattened), and a task instruction addressing the current debater by index.

Signature: `buildRebuttalContext(prompt: string, proposals: Array<{ debater: Debater; output: string }>, rebuttalOutputs: string[], currentDebaterIndex: number): string`

The output includes:
1. The original prompt
2. A '## Proposals' section with each proposal labeled by index and agent name
3. A '## Previous Rebuttals' section (only when rebuttalOutputs is non-empty)
4. A '## Your Task' section identifying the debater by index and providing rebuttal instructions
acceptanceCriteria: buildRebuttalContext() with 2 proposals and 0 rebuttals returns string containing '## Proposals' section with both proposals and no '## Previous Rebuttals' section
buildRebuttalContext() with 2 proposals and 3 rebuttals returns string containing '## Previous Rebuttals' section with all 3 rebuttals numbered
buildRebuttalContext() with currentDebaterIndex 0 returns string containing 'You are debater 1' (1-indexed in output)
buildRebuttalContext() includes each proposal labeled with the debater's agent name
buildRebuttalContext() is exported from src/debate/index.ts barrel

userStories.title: Implement runHybrid() â€” parallel proposals then sequential rebuttals
userStories.description:
Implement `runHybrid()` in `src/debate/session-hybrid.ts`. This is the core hybrid debate flow:

1. Resolve adapters (shared helper from session-helpers.ts)
2. Proposal round â€” parallel via `allSettledBounded`, each debater gets `sessionRole: debate-hybrid-${debaterIndex}` with `keepSessionOpen: true`
3. Rebuttal rounds â€” sequential per-debater stateful turns. For each round r, for each debater d: build rebuttal context via `buildRebuttalContext()` with all proposals + all rebuttals from previous rounds, call `runStatefulTurn()` with same `sessionRole` (resumes ACP session), all turns use `keepSessionOpen: true`
4. After rebuttal loop (or on error), `closeStatefulSession()` for every debater in a `try/finally`
5. Pass proposals as `proposalOutputs` and rebuttals as `critiqueOutputs` to `resolve()`
6. Populate `DebateResult.rebuttals` with per-turn data
7. Log `debate:rebuttal-start` and `debate:rebuttal-end` debug events

Fewer than 2 proposals triggers single-agent fallback (same as panel). Failed rebuttal turns are logged and skipped (resilient). `maxConcurrentDebaters` applies only to proposal round.
acceptanceCriteria: runHybrid() with 2 debaters and rounds: 1 calls runStatefulTurn() for rebuttals exactly 2 times (once per debater) in sequential order
runHybrid() with 3 debaters and rounds: 2 calls runStatefulTurn() for rebuttals exactly 6 times (3 debaters x 2 rounds)
Each rebuttal turn's prompt (via buildRebuttalContext) contains all proposal outputs
Round 2 rebuttal prompts include all round 1 rebuttal outputs in the previous rebuttals section
When a rebuttal turn fails, runHybrid() logs a warning and continues with remaining debaters in that round
When fewer than 2 proposals succeed, runHybrid() returns single-agent fallback result (same as panel stateful mode)
Proposal sessionRole is 'debate-hybrid-${debaterIndex}' and rebuttal sessionRole reuses the same value for the same debater
All runStatefulTurn() calls for rebuttals use keepSessionOpen: true (sessions are closed explicitly after the loop)
After rebuttal loop completes, closeStatefulSession() is called for every debater whose session was opened
When the rebuttal loop throws, closeStatefulSession() is still called for all opened sessions (try/finally guarantee)
Rebuttal turn costs are accumulated into totalCostUsd on the returned DebateResult
DebateResult.rebuttals contains one entry per successful rebuttal turn with correct debater, round, and output
runHybrid() logs 'debate:rebuttal-start' with round and debaterIndex before each rebuttal turn

userStories.title: runHybrid() skeleton and parallel proposal round
userStories.description:
Create `src/debate/session-hybrid.ts` with the `runHybrid()` function. Implement the proposal phase only:

1. Resolve adapters using the shared helper from `session-helpers.ts`
2. Run proposals in parallel via `allSettledBounded`, each debater gets `sessionRole: 'debate-hybrid-${debaterIndex}'` with `keepSessionOpen: true`
3. Collect successful proposal outputs; if fewer than 2 succeed, return the single-agent fallback result (same path as panel stateful mode)
4. `maxConcurrentDebaters` applies to this round only
5. Return proposals ready to be passed as `proposalOutputs` to `resolve()` â€” the rebuttal loop is a stub (implemented in US-004-B)

The rebuttal loop body can be a `TODO` placeholder at this stage.
acceptanceCriteria: sessionRole for each proposal call is 'debate-hybrid-${debaterIndex}' (index 0-based)
All proposal calls are made in parallel via allSettledBounded respecting maxConcurrentDebaters
Every proposal call uses keepSessionOpen: true
When fewer than 2 proposals succeed, runHybrid() returns the single-agent fallback result (same shape as panel stateful fallback)
Successful proposal outputs are collected and available as proposalOutputs for the resolve() call
Adapters are resolved via the shared helper imported from session-helpers.ts (not inline)

routing.reasoning: Creates a new file with ~120 LOC touching 3-4 modules. Proposal-round logic is well-scoped: parallel fan-out with a fallback path. No stateful session lifecycle yet. Fits three-session-tdd-lite.

userStories.title: runHybrid() sequential rebuttal loop, session cleanup, and result assembly
userStories.description:
Extend `runHybrid()` in `src/debate/session-hybrid.ts` (built in US-004-A) with the full rebuttal loop and result assembly:

1. For each round `r` (1..rounds), for each debater `d`: build rebuttal context via `buildRebuttalContext()` (all proposals + all rebuttals from rounds < r), then call `runStatefulTurn()` reusing the same `sessionRole: 'debate-hybrid-${debaterIndex}'` with `keepSessionOpen: true`
2. Failed rebuttal turns are caught, logged as warnings, and skipped â€” loop continues
3. Wrap the entire rebuttal loop in `try/finally`; in the `finally` block call `closeStatefulSession()` for every debater whose session was opened
4. Accumulate per-turn costs into `totalCostUsd` on the returned `DebateResult`
5. Populate `DebateResult.rebuttals` with one entry per successful turn (debater index, round, output)
6. Pass proposals as `proposalOutputs` and collected rebuttals as `critiqueOutputs` to `resolve()`
7. Log `debate:rebuttal-start` (with `round` and `debaterIndex`) before each turn
acceptanceCriteria: With 2 debaters and rounds=1, runStatefulTurn() is called exactly 2 times in sequential debater order
With 3 debaters and rounds=2, runStatefulTurn() is called exactly 6 times (3 debaters Ă— 2 rounds, inner loop is debaters)
Each rebuttal turn's prompt (via buildRebuttalContext) includes all successful proposal outputs
Round 2 rebuttal prompts include all round 1 rebuttal outputs in the previous-rebuttals section
When a rebuttal turn throws, runHybrid() logs a warning and continues with the remaining debaters in that round
Every rebuttal runStatefulTurn() call uses the same sessionRole as the proposal round for that debater and keepSessionOpen: true
After the rebuttal loop completes normally, closeStatefulSession() is called once per opened debater session
When the rebuttal loop throws, closeStatefulSession() is still called for all opened sessions (try/finally guarantee)
Per-turn rebuttal costs are summed and reflected in totalCostUsd on the returned DebateResult
DebateResult.rebuttals contains one entry per successful rebuttal turn with correct debaterIndex, round, and output; debug event 'debate:rebuttal-start' is emitted before each turn

routing.reasoning: ~200 LOC adding nested loop logic with stateful ACP session lifecycle, try/finally cleanup, cost accumulation, and resilient error handling across multiple modules. The try/finally session-close guarantee and round-over-round context threading are subtle correctness requirements that warrant three-session-tdd with strict session isolation.

analysis:
## Key Files & Impact Areas

### Core files requiring modification:
1. **src/debate/session.ts** (873 lines) â€” Main DebateSession class. Currently over 2x the 400-line limit. Contains `run()`, `runOneShot()`, `runStateful()`, `runPlan()`, `resolve()`, plus helper functions (`buildFailedResult`, `runComplete`, `modelTierFromDebater`, `isTierLabel`, `resolveDebaterModel`) and types (`ResolvedDebater`, `SuccessfulProposal`, `ResolveOutcome`, `DebateSessionOptions`). The `_debateSessionDeps` injection point lives here. Must be split before adding `runHybrid()`.
2. **src/debate/types.ts** (102 lines) â€” `DebateStageConfig` interface needs `mode: DebateMode` field. `DebateResult` needs optional `rebuttals?: Rebuttal[]`. New `DebateMode` and `Rebuttal` types needed.
3. **src/config/schemas.ts** (799 lines) â€” `DebateStageConfigSchema` factory (line 466-482) needs `mode` field with `z.enum(['panel', 'hybrid']).default('panel')`. The default function on line 751 also needs `mode` per-stage.
4. **src/debate/prompts.ts** (63 lines) â€” Needs new `buildRebuttalContext()` function.
5. **src/debate/index.ts** (18 lines) â€” Barrel exports need updating for new modules.

### New files to create:
- `src/debate/session-helpers.ts` â€” Extracted shared logic: `buildFailedResult`, `runComplete`, `modelTierFromDebater`, `isTierLabel`, types (`ResolvedDebater`, `SuccessfulProposal`, `ResolveOutcome`), `_debateSessionDeps`, `resolveDebaterModel`
- `src/debate/session-one-shot.ts` â€” Extracted `runOneShot()` as standalone function
- `src/debate/session-stateful.ts` â€” Extracted `runStateful()`, `runStatefulTurn()`, `closeStatefulSession()`
- `src/debate/session-plan.ts` â€” Extracted `runPlan()`
- `src/debate/session-hybrid.ts` â€” New `runHybrid()` implementation

### Existing test files (all must continue passing after refactor):
- `test/unit/config/debate-schema.test.ts` (304 lines) â€” Schema validation tests
- `test/unit/cli/plan-debate.test.ts` (424 lines) â€” Plan debate tests
- `test/unit/cli/plan-decompose-debate.test.ts` (508 lines) â€” Plan decompose debate tests
- `test/unit/verification/rectification-loop-debate.test.ts` (312 lines)
- `test/unit/verification/rectification-loop-debate-cost.test.ts` (134 lines)
- `test/unit/review/semantic-debate.test.ts` (429 lines)

### Architecture observations:
- `DebateSession` uses `_debateSessionDeps` for DI (getAgent, getSafeLogger, readFile) â€” must remain a single injection point
- `run()` currently dispatches to `runOneShot()` or `runStateful()` based on `sessionMode` â€” needs `mode` check first
- `runStatefulTurn()` uses `adapter.run()` with `sessionRole` and `keepSessionOpen` for ACP session management â€” hybrid reuses this
- `allSettledBounded` from `concurrency.ts` handles bounded parallel execution â€” reused for hybrid proposals
- `resolve()` method is private and handles all resolver types â€” shared across all modes
- Barrel imports in `index.ts` re-export `DebateSession`, `_debateSessionDeps`, `DebateSessionOptions` from `session.ts` â€” must not break

### Risks:
1. **Refactor breakage** â€” Splitting 873-line session.ts into 5 files risks breaking imports throughout the codebase. The barrel `index.ts` must maintain identical exports.
2. **Session lifecycle in hybrid** â€” ACP sessions must be properly closed even on errors. The `try/finally` pattern with `closeStatefulSession()` is critical.
3. **Backward compatibility** â€” `mode` defaults to `'panel'` so existing configs are unaffected. The `?? 'one-shot'` fallback for `sessionMode` handles undefined.
4. **Schema changes** â€” Adding `mode` to `DebateStageConfigSchema` affects all 5 stage configs. The default function (line 751) uses inline objects that bypass the schema factory, so both locations need updating.

### Dependencies between stories:
- US-000 (split session.ts) is prerequisite for all others
- US-001 (config) is prerequisite for US-002 (routing) and US-001b (types)
- US-002 (routing) is prerequisite for US-003 (runHybrid)
- US-001b (types) is prerequisite for US-003 (runHybrid)
- US-003 depends on the rebuttal prompt builder, which can be included in US-003 or a prior story
